name: Cravings Filo & Asian Mart
description:
Cravings Filo & Asian Mart is a dedicated Filipino and Asian grocery store located at 645 Warrigal Road in Chadstone, Melbourne. Situated conveniently in front of Holmesglen, the shop provides a welcoming space for the local community to find authentic tastes of home.

The store specialises in stocking a wide variety of essential Philippine goods, ranging from pantry staples and rice to popular snacks, condiments, and toiletries. Visitors can also browse an array of frozen favourites, including traditional ice creams, processed meats, and convenient ready-to-eat Filipino meals.

As an extension of the family-owned Cravings Catering business established in 2014, the mart reflects a deep passion for handcrafted Filipino cuisine. Whether you are looking for specific ingredients to cook a favourite family recipe or searching for convenient catering options for an upcoming event, this friendly neighbourhood shop offers a reliable connection to the rich flavours of the Philippines. It is a fantastic destination for both homesick expats and locals wishing to explore diverse Asian ingredients.

name: El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Melbourne
description:
El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Melbourne is a vibrant Catholic charismatic prayer fellowship that serves the Filipino-Australian Catholic community in western Melbourne. The group holds regular, welcoming prayer meetings and fellowship assemblies at Christ the King Catholic Church in Braybrook, situated on Churchill Avenue.

Attendees appreciate the warm, inclusive, and supportive atmosphere of the gatherings. Community members describe the fellowship as a wonderful place of worship that feels peaceful and comforting. The local parish priests and organisers are highly regarded for their kind, loving, and dedicated service to the diverse congregation of migrants.

Meetings feature regular charismatic praise and worship, prayers, and community support. The group also organises Friday evening adoration sessions, which are highly valued by the community for spiritual reflection. These prayer assemblies run on the third, fourth, and fifth Saturdays of each month from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM at Christ the King in Braybrook. For partners in the northern suburbs, the fellowship also coordinates meetings in Fitzroy North on the first and second Saturdays. The group provides a vital spiritual home and cultural connection for the Filipino diaspora in Victoria.

name: Holy Family Parish Mount Waverley
description:
Holy Family Parish in Mount Waverley is a vibrant Catholic church known for its warm and welcoming atmosphere. Led by Parish Priest Father Justel Callos, the parish serves a diverse local community with dedication and care. Visitors and regular parishioners alike highlight the serene, peaceful environment and the friendly nature of the clergy, staff, and volunteers who support everyone on their spiritual journey.

The parish is closely integrated with the adjacent Holy Family School, fostering a strong sense of unity and family support. This caring environment is highly valued by families, particularly those with unique learning needs.

In addition to standard services, Holy Family Parish is a key gathering point for the local multicultural community. It hosts a dedicated monthly Filipino Mass on the fourth Sunday of each month at 11:30 am, as well as a weekly Korean Mass on Sundays at 1:30 pm. Regular weekend masses are held on Saturday at 5:00 pm, and Sundays at 8:30 am and 10:00 am. Situated at 236 Stephensons Road, the parish offers wheelchair-accessible facilities and a peaceful columbarium memorial garden.

name: Philippine Community Centre
description:
The Philippine Community Centre in Laverton stands as Australia’s first and largest dedicated hub for the Filipino-Australian community. Officially opened in 2024 by the Philippine Fiesta of Victoria Incorporated, this landmark facility provides a welcoming space for cultural celebrations, social gatherings, and private events.

Visitors regularly praise the centre for its excellent amenities, highlighting the spacious multipurpose function hall, clean bathrooms, and fully equipped commercial kitchen. The venue is a popular choice for weddings, parties, and festive celebrations. It features a large foyer and generous parking spaces, making it highly accessible and convenient for large groups. Guests frequently note the warm, vibrant atmosphere and the friendly community spirit that characterises the events hosted here.

Beyond private functions, the centre serves as a vital social anchor, hosting community workshops, sports activities, and cultural programs. By bringing people together, the centre preserves and promotes Filipino heritage while fostering strong connections within the wider local community.

name: Suki Kart
description:
Suki Kart is an online Filipino grocery store that specialises in delivering a wide range of Filipino food staples, frozen products, snacks, and traditional cooking merchandise across Australia. Although their physical retail storefront is located in Hornsby, New South Wales, they provide efficient nationwide shipping that connects the Filipino-Australian community, including Melbourne, with their favourite tastes of home.

Customers highly appreciate Suki Kart for its reliable online ordering system and prompt, friendly customer service. The team is known for being extremely responsive to special delivery instructions and proactive in communicating about stock availability or processing quick refunds. For frozen and perishable items, they utilise high-quality packaging, featuring double silver wrapping and disposable freezer packs to ensure goods arrive in excellent condition.

Their comprehensive inventory features popular snacks, dry goods, frozen meats, and hard-to-find cooking utensils like llanera and polvoron moulds. Suki Kart serves as a convenient one-stop shop for anyone craving authentic Filipino ingredients, bringing the vibrant flavours of the Philippines straight to your doorstep with fast and secure delivery.
